archetype: Automated Taxidermy Workflow Management and Compliance Tracking System
domain: Taxidermy and Natural History Museum Curation
description: This system streamlines the taxidermy process for natural history museums, ensuring compliance with regulations such as the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES) and the Endangered Species Act (ESA). It tracks specimen preparation, mounts, and storage, while also managing permits, licenses, and conservation status. The system must handle complex workflows, integrate with external databases, and ensure data integrity.
rules: MUST comply with CITES and ESA regulations for specimen handling and trade
MUST integrate with museum collection management systems (CMS) for seamless data exchange
MUST support multiple taxidermy techniques and workflows for various specimen types
MUST track and manage hazardous materials and chemicals used in the taxidermy process
MUST provide real-time inventory management and reporting for specimens, mounts, and storage
anti_patterns: DO NOT use generic workflow management tools that lack domain-specific knowledge and integration
DO NOT neglect to incorporate CITES and ESA compliance checks in the workflow
DO NOT overlook the importance of data integrity and backups for sensitive specimen information
key_technical_challenges: Developing a flexible and adaptable workflow engine to accommodate various taxidermy techniques and specimen types
Integrating with museum CMS systems and external databases while ensuring data consistency and integrity
Implementing robust security measures to protect sensitive specimen information and comply with regulations

recommended_stack.language: Python - due to its extensive libraries and frameworks for data manipulation and workflow management
recommended_stack.framework: Django - for its robust ORM and support for complex workflows
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL - for its advanced data integrity features and support for GIS data
recommended_stack.cache: Redis - for its high-performance caching capabilities
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ - for its flexibility and support for complex workflows
